name: City of Burgos — circular through the capital of the Province of Burgos
name_original: Ciudad de Burgos — circular por la capital de la Provincia de Burgos
region_intro: Jogging routes around Arlanzon feature diverse landscapes, from tranquil riverbanks to scenic greenways and wooded areas. The region is characterized by the Arlanzon River, offering several promenades, and the Embalse de Arlanzón, with its adjacent Sierra de la Demanda Greenway. These natural features provide a blend of accessible, picturesque paths and more varied trails for running. Best jogging routes around Arlanzon The most popular jogging route is City of Burgos — circular through the capital of the Province of Burgos, a 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail that takes 30 minutes to complete. This easy route offers a pleasant run through the city's surroundings. Another top favourite among local runners is Camino de Santiago — circular by Rabé de la Calzada, a difficult 9.4 miles (15.1 km) path. This route follows a section of the historic Camino de Santiago, providing a challenging and scenic experience. Local runners also love the Mecerreyes along the Camino del Cid — circular from Cuevas de San Clemente, a 8.2 miles (13.2 km) trail leading through historical paths, often completed in about 1 hour 39 minutes. Jogging around Arlanzon is defined by river promenades, greenways, and wooded areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside runs to more challenging trails. The routes in Arlanzon are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 4000 runners have used komoot to explore Arlanzon's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Arlanzon?
A: Arlanzon offers a wide variety of running routes, with over 270 trails available. These range from easy riverside paths to more challenging trails through varied terrain, catering to all levels of runners.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Arlanzon?
A: Yes, Arlanzon has several easy running routes perfect for beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051918166/city-of-burgos-circular-through-the-capital-of-the-province-of-burgos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City of Burgos — circular through the capital of the Province of Burgos&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.8-mile (4.5 km) trail that offers a pleasant run through the city's surroundings.

Q: Where can I find longer running trails for endurance training in Arlanzon?
A: For those seeking longer runs, Arlanzon provides several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050108949/mecerreyes-along-the-camino-del-cid-circular-from-cuevas-de-san-clemente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mecerreyes along the Camino del Cid — circular from Cuevas de San Clemente&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.2-mile (13.2 km) trail, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051971060/camino-de-santiago-circular-by-rabe-de-la-calzada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Santiago — circular by Rabé de la Calzada&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 9.4-mile (15.1 km) path, ideal for endurance.

Q: Are the running trails around Arlanzon dog-friendly?
A: Many of the natural paths and green spaces along the Arlanzon River, such as the promenades and parks like Fuentes Blancas, are generally dog-friendly. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and respect local regulations, especially in more populated areas or nature reserves.

Q: Are there family-friendly jogging paths in the Arlanzon area?
A: Absolutely. The Arlanzon River offers several promenades like La Isla and Paseo del Empecinado, which are flat and scenic, making them perfect for family jogs. Fuentes Blancas Park also provides extensive green spaces and recreation areas suitable for families.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Arlanzon?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Arlanzon are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33813908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa Fluvial de Fuente Prior loop from Polígono Río Vena&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14948920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de los Delfines loop from Villa Pilar&lt;/a&gt;, which provide varied distances and scenery.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while jogging in Arlanzon?
A: Jogging in Arlanzon offers diverse scenery. You can run along the picturesque Arlanzon River promenades, explore the botanical beauty of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5774186" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rosette Monument, Paseo de la Isla&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy the expansive green spaces of Fuentes Blancas Park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2729971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atapuerca Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt; area also presents unique geological formations and landscapes.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Arlanzon?
A: The running routes in Arlanzon are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse landscapes, from tranquil riverbanks and greenways to wooded areas, and the variety of options for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by historical sites or castles?
A: Yes, some routes offer glimpses of historical significance. While jogging, you might pass by areas with views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3536807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Staircase to Castillo de Burgos&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2052241" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;city of Burgos&lt;/a&gt; itself, which is rich in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14944422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Solar del Cid loop from Burgos&lt;/a&gt; also offers a connection to historical paths.

Q: What is the best season for running in Arlanzon?
A: Arlanzon offers pleasant running conditions throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn provide mild temperatures and lush scenery, ideal for enjoying the riverbanks and wooded areas. Summer mornings and evenings are also popular, with shaded routes offering respite from the sun. Winters can be cooler but still suitable for running, especially on clear days.

Q: Are there less crowded running routes available in Arlanzon?
A: While popular promenades can see more activity, the region's extensive network of trails means you can often find quieter paths. Exploring routes further from the immediate city center, such as those leading towards the Embalse de Arlanzón or through the Sierra de Atapuerca, can offer a more solitary running experience.

Q: Can I find running routes with good parking access in Arlanzon?
A: Many of the running routes, especially those along the Arlanzon River and in larger parks like Fuentes Blancas, have accessible parking nearby. For routes starting further afield, such as those connecting to the Sierra de la Demanda Greenway, specific parking areas are usually designated for outdoor enthusiasts.
tips:
Located on top of a hill. In the year 884 Count Diego Porcelos founded the city and this location served as a surveillance post for the Christian territories.
The importance of the Castle grew along with that of the city until a lavish royal fortress was built inside it. The siege it suffered during the war of the Castilian succession was especially relevant, in which the supporters of the infanta Juana la Beltraneja resisted for several months the attacks of the troops defending the throne of Isabella the Catholic.
A good climb that was worth it for the views from the viewpoint.
